skill.name: churn-prevention
skill.description: Helps subscription teams reduce voluntary and involuntary churn through cancel flows, save offers, health scoring, and payment recovery playbooks.
skill.summary: Design churn prevention systems for SaaS cancellation, retention, and failed payment recovery.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, command execution, and path traversal patterns. Manual review found the weak cryptography and command execution alerts are false positives caused by Markdown examples, code-formatted labels, and business terms; no executable scripts, network calls, or prompt injection evidence were found. A low filesystem concern remains because the skill asks agents to read local marketing context and links to documentation outside the skill directory.

low_findings.title: Local Context File Read
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to read .agents/product-marketing-context.md or .claude/product-marketing-context.md before asking questions. This is relevant to the skill, but it can expose local business context to the model, so users should know the skill reads project files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line explicitly asks the agent to read local project context files. It is a legitimate workflow step, but it creates a real local data access consideration.

low_findings.title: Cross-Directory Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The skill includes ../../ links to tool documentation outside the skill directory. These links are not writes or hidden traversal, but following them can read files outside the package boundary.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations contain visible relative documentation links with ../.. path traversal sequences. The surrounding text is advisory, not executable, which limits severity.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited locations are Markdown code fences, UI mockups, tables, or code-formatted tool names. No shell command execution, Ruby backticks, or user-controlled command construction was found in these files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows documentation formatting and examples rather than executable source. The package contains Markdown and JSON only, with no scripts that run these snippets.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithms, but the cited lines contain churn guidance, eval prompts, Markdown links, or business terminology. No hashing, encryption, signature verification, or crypto API usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged files are Markdown guidance and eval JSON, and the cited lines do not call cryptographic APIs. The alert is a keyword collision, not a semantic crypto risk.

content.user_title: Reduce SaaS Churn With Save Flows
content.value_statement: Subscription teams lose revenue when cancellation and payment recovery are handled with generic flows. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design targeted cancel flows, save offers, dunning sequences, and churn risk models.
actual_capabilities: Designs cancel flows with survey, save offer, confirmation, and post-cancel steps.
Maps cancellation reasons to discounts, pauses, downgrades, support escalation, or no-offer exits.
Creates payment recovery plans with pre-dunning, smart retries, dunning emails, and grace periods.
Defines churn health scores using usage, billing, support, and engagement signals.
Recommends retention metrics, save-rate tracking, cohort analysis, and A/B test ideas.
Provides provider-aware guidance for Stripe, Chargebee, Paddle, Recurly, and Braintree.
limitations: It does not implement billing provider APIs or change subscription settings directly.
It relies on the user to provide churn, billing, and product usage data.
Benchmarks and legal notes should be checked against current company policy and local regulations.
Full win-back email sequence writing is delegated to a separate email-sequence workflow.

use_cases.title: Launch a cancel flow
use_cases.description: Create a structured cancellation journey with an exit survey, targeted save offer, clear confirmation, and analytics plan.

use_cases.title: Recover failed payments
use_cases.description: Plan dunning timing, retry logic, card update reminders, and customer messaging for involuntary churn.

use_cases.title: Prioritize at-risk accounts
use_cases.description: Build a health score model and intervention tiers for customers showing churn signals before they cancel.

prompt_templates.title: Basic churn diagnosis
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: identify the main churn problem.
prompt_templates.prompt: Our product has [monthly churn rate] churn with [number] subscribers at [price]. Ask for any missing context, then recommend the first three churn prevention improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Cancel flow design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a cancel flow for a [B2B or B2C] SaaS product. Include exit survey options, save offers for each reason, confirmation copy, and metrics to track.

prompt_templates.title: Dunning recovery plan
prompt_templates.prompt: We lose customers because payments fail. Build a dunning plan for [billing provider] with pre-dunning, retry timing, emails, grace period rules, and recovery metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced retention system
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: connect churn prediction, interventions, and experiments.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a churn prevention operating system for [product type]. Include health score signals, risk tiers, intervention playbooks, cancel-flow experiments, and reporting cadence.

output_examples.input: A $49 per month project management SaaS has 7 percent monthly churn and no cancel flow.
output: A prioritized plan that separates voluntary and involuntary churn.
A cancel flow with survey, dynamic offer, confirmation, and post-cancel follow-up.
A measurement plan for save rate, offer acceptance, dunning recovery, and churn by cohort.

output_examples.input: Fifteen percent of churn comes from expired or declined credit cards.
output: A pre-dunning schedule for card expiration reminders.
Retry rules by decline type and billing provider capability.
A four-message dunning sequence with grace period and pause rules.

output_examples.input: The team wants to identify churn risk before customers cancel.
output: A customer health score using product usage, support, billing, and engagement signals.
Risk tiers with recommended outreach for healthy, needs attention, at risk, and critical accounts.
Data sources and implementation steps for the first version.

best_practices: Start with a clear cancellation path and one relevant save offer per reason.
Track saved customers beyond the first month to measure real retention impact.
Separate voluntary churn, involuntary churn, and unavoidable churn in reporting.
anti_patterns: Do not hide the cancel button or make cancellation harder than signup.
Do not offer the same discount to every cancelling customer.
Do not treat a short-term discount acceptance as a permanent save.

faq.question: What types of churn does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers voluntary churn from cancellations and involuntary churn from failed payments.

faq.question: Can it write complete win-back email campaigns?
faq.answer: It provides churn-specific timing and strategy, but full win-back email writing belongs in an email sequence workflow.

faq.question: Does it integrate directly with Stripe or Chargebee?
faq.answer: No. It gives provider-aware setup guidance, but it does not call billing APIs or change account settings.

faq.question: What data should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare churn rate, subscriber count, average revenue, billing provider, cancel reasons, and product usage signals.

faq.question: Can it help with legal compliance?
faq.answer: It highlights cancellation and data retention considerations, but legal teams should validate requirements for each market.

faq.question: Is this useful for early-stage SaaS companies?
faq.answer: Yes. It can start with a simple survey, one save offer, payment reminders, and basic churn metrics.
